client_focus: Out-of-school K-8 programs and 6-week summer enrichment programs
research_scope: Current grant opportunities, application processes, deadlines, and contact information for education nonprofits

foundations.foundation_name: THE MAXIMILIAN E AND MARION O HOFFMAN FOUNDATION

current_opportunities.name: Education and Community Support Grants
current_opportunities.focus: Education, health, human services in central Connecticut and Hartford area

how_to_apply.portal_url: Contact foundation directly for application form
requirements: Description of proposed work and how it will be conducted
Names and CVs of project staff
Organization legal and tax status documentation
how_to_apply.notes: Application form must be obtained directly from the foundation

deadlines.notes: No published fixed deadlines; contact foundation for review schedule

current_priorities: Community development in West Hartford area

contact.address: 970 Farmington Ave Ste 203, West Hartford, CT 06107-2134

fit_assessment.notes: Connecticut-based foundation with strong geographic alignment (CT focus). Median grant $20,000 aligns with mid-size education nonprofits. 94.7% of giving is in CT, making this a strong fit for Connecticut-based Horizons National. Typical recipients are organizations with budgets $662K-$7.5M (Horizons fits profile).
red_flags: Declining giving trend (-16.1% over 2020-2023)
Low repeat funding rate (12.7%) suggests one-time funding more likely than multi-year support
Unclear application process - no online application portal found; must contact directly

current_opportunities.name: JustFilms
current_opportunities.focus: Documentary film production and free expression

how_to_apply.process: Unsolicited proposals NOT accepted by mail, email, or phone. Only eligible applicants to designated opportunity competitions can apply.
how_to_apply.portal_url: Submit through designated program portals only when open
requirements: Alignment with Ford Foundation strategic focus areas
Application to specific open opportunity programs ONLY
Names and CVs of project staff
how_to_apply.notes: Ford Foundation rarely accepts unsolicited proposals. Organizations must apply to specific open opportunities listed on their website.

dates: NYC Good Neighbor: Late Summer/Early Fall 2025
Other programs: Check website for periodic opportunities
deadlines.notes: Most programs are invited/invitation-only. Monitor website for new opportunities.

fit_assessment.notes: Large national funder with significant assets ($16.8B). Education programs exist but are primarily higher-ed focused and K-8 enrichment is not a primary program area. Median grant $100K is above Horizons typical ask range. Very selective - rarely accepts unsolicited proposals. Strong geographic challenge: foundation in NY, giving concentrated in NY.
red_flags: Does not accept unsolicited proposals - must be invited to specific programs
No K-8 enrichment programs identified; education focus is primarily higher education and diversity in academia
Geographic focus is NY (27% of giving), not CT
High effort to secure: very competitive, limited open opportunities
Last major education program (Ford Fellowships) ended in 2025

current_opportunities.name: Community Funding RFP (Grantee re-granting program)
current_opportunities.deadline: RFP published on website; check for current deadline
current_opportunities.focus: Youth and Families, Economic Mobility in Florida (Miami-Dade, Broward, Palm Beach counties)

current_opportunities.name: Education and Nursing Programs
current_opportunities.focus: Higher education, nursing programs, education initiatives in CT

how_to_apply.process: Organization must respond to specific RFPs. See website for current open funding opportunities.
requirements: Geographic eligibility (primarily Florida focus, some CT focus)
Alignment with Youth & Families or Economic Mobility portfolios
how_to_apply.notes: DeLuca does NOT accept unsolicited applications. Must respond to published RFPs. Limited K-12 education focus; stronger in higher ed and healthcare.

dates: Check delucafdn.org for current RFP deadline
deadlines.notes: No published fixed deadlines for regular giving; deadlines tied to RFPs

contact.address: 49 N Federal Highway 146, Pompano Beach, FL 33062-4304

fit_assessment.notes: Major Connecticut funder ($9.8M+ grants to CT organizations). Strong education and youth focus. Founded by DeLuca who grew up in Connecticut and has invested $40M+ in Connecticut education. Grant amounts ($125K median, up to $9.8M) significantly exceed Horizons typical ask range. Growing giving trend (+412% change 2022-2023 in raw data).
red_flags: Does NOT accept unsolicited applications; must wait for RFP
Geographic focus is primarily Florida (Pompano Beach HQ), though significant Connecticut giving exists
Grant sizes ($125K+ median) are much larger than Horizons typical ask; may be overqualified
No published application process or deadlines found
Does not appear to have K-8 enrichment as explicit priority (higher ed focus)

foundations.foundation_name: REEVES H J UW FBO GIRLS INC ET

current_opportunities.name: Girls Inc. Support - Unrestricted General Support
current_opportunities.focus: Girls Inc. organizations and youth serving nonprofits in CT (primarily)

how_to_apply.process: Limited information publicly available. Contact Bank of America trustee for information. Trust historically funds Girls Inc. and youth organizations.
how_to_apply.notes: This is a charitable trust (4947(a)(1) non-exempt trust) administered through Bank of America. Very limited public application information available.

deadlines.notes: No fixed deadline; review likely quarterly or as needed

fit_assessment.notes: Small trust ($28.8K annual giving) focused on Girls Inc. and youth organizations in Connecticut. While Horizons is CT-based and youth-focused, grant amounts are very small ($5.5K median). 100% repeat funding rate (only 5 grantees historically funded repeatedly) suggests limited capacity for new grantees.
red_flags: Very small grant amounts ($4.2K-$6.5K) far below Horizons needs
100% repeat funding rate indicates extremely limited capacity for new grantees
No published application process
Declining giving trend (-16.4% 2022-2024)
Website appears incorrect/unrelated (earthcam.com domain)
Very limited public information - appears to be quasi-dormant trust

foundations.foundation_name: BRUCE N GRIFFING TRUST U W POR 3

current_opportunities.name: Youth Programs in Derby and Shelton, CT
current_opportunities.focus: Youth development, education, early childhood, arts/culture, health in Derby and Shelton, CT

requirements: Located in or serving Derby or Shelton, Connecticut
how_to_apply.notes: Year-round submissions accepted online; March 15 deadline for annual funding cycle

dates: March 15 - Annual deadline for spring/summer grant cycle
Year-round submission accepted, but March 15 required for May decision
deadlines.notes: Decisions communicated by May. Can submit anytime but March 15 ensures review in current cycle.

current_priorities: Youth development and education in Derby and Shelton

fit_assessment.notes: Strong geographic mismatch is the primary concern. Trust was established to benefit Derby and Shelton, Connecticut youth ONLY. Horizons National operates out of Connecticut but serves multiple cities and has national scope (6-week summer program attracts students from broader region). Average grant $5K-$7.5K is below Horizons typical grant ask. Trust is stable (not declining) with reasonable repeat funding rate (40%).
red_flags: CRITICAL: Geographic restriction to ONLY Derby and Shelton, CT - Horizons may not serve these towns exclusively
Small grant amounts ($1K-$11.5K) below Horizons needs ($25K-$75K range)
Only 5 grants per year awarded (very limited capacity)
Limited opportunity if Horizons not directly serving Derby and Shelton
Website verification needed - Wells Fargo administers but information incomplete

research_notes.methodology: Web search and website fetch of foundation websites, Foundation Directory (Candid), CauseIQ, and ProPublica Nonprofit Explorer
research_notes.data_freshness: Most foundation data from 2023-2024 tax filings; website information current as of 2025-12-31
limitations: No direct contact with foundations; information sourced from public databases
Some foundations (Reeves, Griffing Trust) have very limited public application information
Specific 2025 RFP deadlines for DeLuca and Ford require checking their websites in real-time
Contact emails not publicly available for most foundations - phone numbers provided where available

strongest_fits: THE MAXIMILIAN E AND MARION O HOFFMAN FOUNDATION (EIN: 222648036) - CT-based, education focus, appropriate grant sizes, accepting applications
BRUCE N GRIFFING TRUST (EIN: 066020281) - Clear application process via CyberGrants, published deadline (March 15), CT focus
moderate_fits: ELISABETH C DELUCA FOUNDATION INC (EIN: 301214067) - Major CT funder but very selective, RFP-based, grant sizes typically larger than Horizons ask
weaker_fits: THE FORD FOUNDATION (EIN: 131684331) - Does not accept unsolicited proposals, NY-focused, higher ed emphasis
REEVES H J UW FBO GIRLS INC ET (EIN: 066334519) - Very small grant amounts, nearly 100% repeat funding rate limits new grantees
recommended_next_steps: Priority 1: Contact Hoffman Foundation (860-521-2949) to request application form and clarify process
Priority 2: Verify Griffing Trust geographic eligibility - confirm Horizons serves Derby and/or Shelton, CT
Priority 3: Check DeLuca Foundation website for current RFP opportunities and deadlines
Priority 4: Monitor Ford Foundation website for NYC Good Neighbor or other education program openings late summer 2025
Research Note: Reeves foundation appears largely dormant; lowest priority for outreach
